text.title: Structured Logging as Code Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Structured Logging as Code methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every log line MUST be JSON with the required fields {ts, level, msg, service, env, request_id}; prose / unstructured lines are rejected by the ingestion contract.
rule.rationale: Indexable logs require a stable shape; prose lines force grep + regex, which collapses under volume.

rule.statement: PII fields (email, phone, full name, payment token, IP) MUST pass through a redaction layer before serialization; unredacted fields are rejected by the CI parser fixture.
rule.rationale: Logs are a backup PII source; unredacted logs make every audit a discovery event.

rule.statement: Every log line in a request scope MUST carry `trace_id` and `span_id` from the OTel context; missing trace context on request-scoped logs is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without trace correlation cross-service debugging falls back to timestamp matching which is unreliable past 100 req/s.

rule.statement: Log levels MUST follow the matrix: dev=DEBUG, staging=INFO with SAMPLED DEBUG, prod=INFO with SAMPLED DEBUG (1%); broad DEBUG in prod is rejected.
rule.rationale: DEBUG-in-prod blows cost + masks signal; sampled DEBUG keeps the diagnostic path open without burning budget.

rule.statement: CI MUST run a parser fixture that consumes sample log lines, validates the JSON Schema, and asserts redaction; regressions block merge.
rule.rationale: Without a parser fixture, log shape silently drifts and breaks downstream queries; CI gate keeps the contract stable.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if every entry in `## Applies If` holds and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: Misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; explicit skip rule is the universal default branch.
